codeReview.summary: All three client integration docs (OPENCODE.md, OH-MY-PI.md, FACTORY-DROID.md) exist and contain substantial content with setup instructions, configuration examples, and troubleshooting sections. However, there is a blocking issue: the health endpoint response examples are incomplete and don't match the actual implementation response format.

issues.description: Health endpoint response example shows `{"status":"ok","version":"2.3.20","auth":"not_authenticated"}` but actual implementation (src/proxy/standalone-server.ts:902-911) returns additional `mcp` field: `{status, version, auth, mcp: {enabled, servers, tools}}`. Users may think something is wrong when they see additional fields in the actual response.
Same issue: health endpoint response example is incomplete, missing the `mcp` field that the actual implementation returns.
Same issue: health endpoint response example is incomplete, missing the `mcp` field.

Environment variable table lists `TOOL_LOOP_MAX_REPEAT` with default 2, but this only applies to standalone-server mode. When running as OpenCode plugin, the env var is `CURSOR_ACP_TOOL_LOOP_MAX_REPEAT` (src/plugin.ts:301) with default 3 (src/plugin.ts:1795). Users running in plugin mode may be confused when this env var doesn't work.

summary: Documentation exists for all three target clients with comprehensive setup instructions, config examples, and troubleshooting. However, the health endpoint response examples are inaccurate across all three docs - they don't show the `mcp` field that the actual implementation returns. This is a blocking issue that will cause confusion during user testing. The environment variable naming inconsistency between plugin mode and standalone mode should also be documented.
